title: Gmail OAuth fails with 403 error for read-only access
symptom: When attempting to authenticate Gmail with read-only scopes, a 403 error occurs if additional scopes are not accepted.
root_cause: The built-in Gmail credential in n8n automatically adds additional scopes like gmail.modify and gmail.compose, which are not compatible with a read-only access request. If these scopes are not accepted, Google returns a 403 error.
fix_steps: Go to Credentials → New → OAuth2 API
Fill in the Auth URL, Access Token URL, Client ID, and Client Secret from your Google Cloud project
Set access_type to 'offline' and prompt to 'consent' in OAuth options
agent_instruction: Create a new OAuth2 API credential with the specified settings to control requested scopes.
